items.title: DevOps
items.summary: This chart contains reusable templates called named templates as they can be invoked in other charts by name. The structure and content of the chart are based on the output from a helm create command. File _helpers.tpl is an autogenerated file that contains functions that defne best practices for naming conventions, for example : _. is used to prevent Helm from trying to create manifests using that specific chart.

items.title: Source: config-server/templates/configmap_from_file.yaml
items.summary: Is designed to create ConfigMaps from files in the folder config-repo. A ConfigMap will contain all non-sensitive configuration required by a specific Deployment. The ConfigMap will be mounted as a volume in the Pod that runs the Deployment. The Pod will read the configuration from the ConfigMap as files in its local filesystem. Ex. _configmap_from_file.yaml explaination: Ex. output:

items.title: DevOps
items.summary: Is designed to create Secrets defined by values like credentials provided by the environments(ex. dev-env) The Secrets will be mapped as environment variables in the Pods The template is designed to create multiple Secret manifests using the range function in Helm. Ex. _secret.yaml Each key/value pair is assigned by the range function to the variables $key and $val. The value in the $val variable is piped to the b64enc function to get it properly Base64-encoded as required by a Secret manifest.
items.searchText: devops is designed to create secrets defined by values like credentials provided by the environments(ex. dev-env) the secrets will be mapped as environment variables in the pods the template is designed to create multiple secret manifests using the range function in helm. ex. _secret.yaml each key/value pair is assigned by the range function to the variables $key and $val. the value in the $val variable is piped to the b64enc function to get it properly base64-encoded as required by a secret manifest. dev devops helm common template secret template secret template

items.title: DevOps
items.summary: The Service template introduces support for overriding default values from the common chart, with values specific to the charts that use the common chart. Usually the common chart provides default values for the service type and what port will it be exposed on, but you can override these values through the values.yaml file of the chart of the specific microservice. Ex. _service.yaml
items.searchText: devops the service template introduces support for overriding default values from the common chart, with values specific to the charts that use the common chart. usually the common chart provides default values for the service type and what port will it be exposed on, but you can override these values through the values.yaml file of the chart of the specific microservice. ex. _service.yaml dev devops helm common template service template service template

items.title: Environment Chart
items.summary: Contains the charts for the different environments(ex. dev-env, prod-env). These Charts.yaml file contains dependencies on both the common chart and the charts in the components folder The template folder contains a secrets.yaml template to create environment-specific credentials as Secrets. It is based on the named template for Secrets from the common chart and looks like this:
items.searchText: environment chart contains the charts for the different environments(ex. dev-env, prod-env). these charts.yaml file contains dependencies on both the common chart and the charts in the components folder the template folder contains a secrets.yaml template to create environment-specific credentials as secrets. it is based on the named template for secrets from the common chart and looks like this: dev devops helm environment environment chart

items.title: Helm
items.summary: Is used in a Kubernetes environment to bundle and configure the microservices for deployments. A package is known as a chart. A chart contains templates, default values for the templates and optional dependencies. Each component that needs to be deployed will have its own chart describing how to deploy it. Library charts named common are used to reduce the boilerplate definitions from the component charts. A library chart doesn't contain any deployable definitions but only reusable templates. To deploy all components into different types of environments the concept of parent charts and subcharts will be used. Each environment will be implemented as a parent chart that depends on different sets of subcharts. Helm charts can be published to a helm chart repository.
items.searchText: helm is used in a kubernetes environment to bundle and configure the microservices for deployments. a package is known as a chart. a chart contains templates, default values for the templates and optional dependencies. each component that needs to be deployed will have its own chart describing how to deploy it. library charts named common are used to reduce the boilerplate definitions from the component charts. a library chart doesn't contain any deployable definitions but only reusable templates. to deploy all components into different types of environments the concept of parent charts and subcharts will be used. each environment will be implemented as a parent chart that depends on different sets of subcharts. helm charts can be published to a helm chart repository. dev devops helm helm helm chart structure commands

items.title: MergeSort
items.summary: It uses two temporary arrays leftArray and rightArray to hold the data, then combines them by comparing the elements.

headings.text: Description: Split the array into two halves and recursively sort them. Then merge the two sorted halves.
Time Complexity: O(nlogn) in all cases
Recommended use: When you need a stable sort and have a large dataset.
